title: RNA Extraction and RT-PCR on Mouse Tissues
author: Jayshen Arudkumar

steps.title: Keep the tissue samples on ice
steps.instruction: Keep the tissue samples on ice

steps.title: Set the centrifuge to cool at 4°C
steps.instruction: Set the centrifuge to cool at 4°C

steps.title: Mince the heart and skeletal muscle...
steps.instruction: Mince the heart and skeletal muscle tissues in a few drops of water and place the minced portions into a magNA lyser tube for bead homogenisation step

steps.title: Homogenise samples in the Precellys bead-based...
steps.instruction: Homogenise samples in the Precellys bead-based homogeniser at 6500 RPM for 2 cycles at 20s each

steps.title: Add 500 μL of Invitrogen TRIzol reagent to the supernatant
steps.instruction: Add 500 μL of Invitrogen TRIzol reagent to the supernatant

steps.title: Incubate for 5 minutes at room...
steps.instruction: Incubate for 5 minutes at room temperature (RT) to dissociate the nucleoprotein complex

steps.title: Add 100 μL chloroform Use 0
steps.instruction: Add 100 μL chloroform Use 0.2 ml chloroform/ 1 ml TRIZOL reagent

steps.title: Shake the tubes vigorously by hand...
steps.instruction: Shake the tubes vigorously by hand for 15 seconds to mix

steps.title: Incubate for 2 min at RT
steps.instruction: Incubate for 2 min at RT.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 12,000 RCF in a...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 12,000 RCF in a 4°C cooled centrifuge for 15 minutes.

steps.title: Label new microcentrifuge tubes and keep on ice
steps.instruction: Label new microcentrifuge tubes and keep on ice

steps.title: Transfer approximately 175 μL of the...
steps.instruction: Transfer approximately 175 μL of the colourless liquid layer to a new eppendorf tube.

steps.title: Add an equal volume of 70% ethanol
steps.instruction: Add an equal volume of 70% ethanol. Mix by pipetting. Invert the tube and mix to disperse any visible precipitate that can form after adding ethanol.

steps.title: Transfer a maximum of 700 μL...
steps.instruction: Transfer a maximum of 700 μL to the QIAGEN spin column with a collection tube.

steps.title: Centrifuge for 30 seconds at 8,000 RCF (room temperature centrifuge)
steps.instruction: Centrifuge for 30 seconds at 8,000 RCF (room temperature centrifuge).

steps.title: Place the flow-through back into the...
steps.instruction: Place the flow-through back into the spin column and centrifuge again for 30 seconds at 6,500 RCF. Discard the flow-through and place the column back into the collection tube.

steps.title: Add 700 μL Buffer RW1 to the RNeasy spin column
steps.instruction: Add 700 μL Buffer RW1 to the RNeasy spin column. Centrifuge for 15 seconds at 8,000 RCF.

steps.title: Add 500 μL Buffer RPE to the RNeasy spin column
steps.instruction: Add 500 μL Buffer RPE to the RNeasy spin column. Centrifuge for 15 seconds at 8,000 RCF.

steps.title: Discard the flow-through and place the...
steps.instruction: Discard the flow-through and place the spin column in a new collection tube.

steps.title: Add 500 μL Buffer RPE to the RNeasy spin column
steps.instruction: Add 500 μL Buffer RPE to the RNeasy spin column. Centrifuge for 2 minutes at 8,000 RCF to wash the spin column membrane.

steps.title: Discard the flow-through and place the...
steps.instruction: Discard the flow-through and place the spin column in a new collection tube.

steps.title: Place the RNeasy spin column in a new collection tube
steps.instruction: Place the RNeasy spin column in a new collection tube. Centrifuge for 1 minute at max speed.

steps.title: Place the RNeasy spin column in...
steps.instruction: Place the RNeasy spin column in a new QIAGEN RNase-free 1.5 ml tube.

steps.title: Add 30-50 μL RNase-free water directly...
steps.instruction: Add 30-50 μL RNase-free water directly to the spin column membrane.

steps.title: Centrifuge for 1 minute at 8,000...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge for 1 minute at 8,000 RCF to elute the RNA. For <100 μg starting tissue, use one 30-100 μL elution volume. For >100 μg, use 2–3 sequential 100 μL elutions.

steps.title: Measure concentration of the RNA solution...
steps.instruction: Measure concentration of the RNA solution using NanoDrop Spectrophotometer The A260/A280 ratio should be approximately 2.0, but values between 1.8 and 2.1 are considered to be of acceptable purity.

steps.title: Follow the protocol provided by the...
steps.instruction: Follow the protocol provided by the High-Capacity cDNA Reverse Transcription Kit (Applied Biosystems) to convert 1-2 μg of RNA into cDNA

steps.title: Dilute cDNA in 50 μL ultrapure...
steps.instruction: Dilute cDNA in 50 μL ultrapure water prior to running downstream analysis

steps.title: The New England Biolabs (NEB) website...
steps.instruction: The New England Biolabs (NEB) website can be consulted for the finer details of reaction volumes and components when amplifying using a standard NEB Taq DNA Polymerase and the 10X Standard Taq Reaction Buffer. The primers we used are listed in Supplementary Figure S2. We recommend using cDNA synthesised from 1-2 μg of RNA, as well as assembling all rection components on ice prior to transferring to the preheated thermocycler. After the RT-PCR, confirm the expected band sizes by carefully visualizing the amplified products on a 1% agarose gel.
